engine_state: OFF (APF rule disabled — natural response baseline)
har_mode: DevTools Export HAR (sanitized) — strips request/response bodies but PRESERVES _webSocketMessages frames
subprotocol: SignalR JSON Hub Protocol v1 — frames separated by 0x1e (record separator)
frame_sequence_summary: 3 c2s CHAT INVOCATION type:4 target:chat invocationId:"0" (StreamInvocation, 2580 bytes)
4 s2c type:1 target:update {nonce, requestId, throttling}  (init frame)
5 s2c type:1 target:update {cursor, messages, nonce, requestId} (full text snapshot)
6-9 s2c type:1 target:update {writeAtCursor, nonce} (4 delta chunks)
10 s2c type:1 target:update {messages, nonce, isLastUpdate:true, requestId}
12 s2c COMPLETION type:2 invocationId:"0" item:{messages, conversationId, requestId, telemetry, result, ...}

iter5_critical_fixes.handshake_format: Send {"protocol":"json","version":1} + 0x1e BEFORE any typed envelope. Wait for {} + 0x1e ack from server.
iter5_critical_fixes.frame_separator: 0x1e (chr(30)), append after EVERY JSON object (handshake AND every typed frame)
iter5_critical_fixes.chat_envelope_type: type:4 (StreamInvocation), NOT type:1. target:"chat", invocationId:"0" (string literal '0' for first invocation)
iter5_critical_fixes.content_chunk_path: s2c type:1 target:"update" → arguments[0].messages[*].text (full snapshot or final) OR arguments[0].writeAtCursor.text (incremental delta — append at cursor position)
iter5_critical_fixes.correlation_id_field: invocationId at TOP LEVEL of both c2s (type:4) and s2c (type:2, type:3) envelopes. First chat invocation always uses invocationId:"0"
iter5_critical_fixes.completion_marker: type:2 with invocationId matching c2s. Final bot answer at item.messages[?author="bot"].text
iter5_critical_fixes.stop_condition: Watch for arguments[0].isLastUpdate==true on type:1 update frame, OR arrival of type:2 completion
iter5_critical_fixes.prior_iter4_root_cause: Frame format mismatch — likely sent type:1 invocation instead of type:4, or used wrong frame separator, causing M365SignalrHandler dispatch to fail silently (server closes WS or sends 'Something went wrong').

notes: Engine OFF baseline successfully captured. Page rendered natural answer '1 + 1 = 2' (verified in screenshot). DevTools Network 'Save all as HAR with content' was unavailable in context menu, but DevTools toolbar 'Export HAR (sanitized)' DID preserve _webSocketMessages arrays (sanitized strips HTTP request/response bodies only, not WS frames). 14 WS messages captured from substrate.office.com hub (handshake → invocation → 7 stream chunks → completion → ack → telemetry). JWT access_token in URL redacted in extract_1176.json. iter5 build_m365_signalr_envelope() must use type:4 StreamInvocation with target:'chat' invocationId:'0', 0x1e frame separator, and preceding handshake exchange.
